content.full_text:
Fern Polrrrcal PRACrrcEs CouurssroN
428 J Street r Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA g5gl4_2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (9t6) 322_0886
January 6,2009
Daniel J. McHugh,Esq.
City Attorney
City of Redlands
P.O. Box 3005
Redlands, CA 92373-1505
Re
Dear Mr. McHugh:
This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of City of Redlands
Councilmember Jerry 
-Bgan regarding ihe conflict-of-interest provisions of the political
Reform Act (the "Act").' Because your question seeks g.n"rul guidance and is not
limited to a specifi"F:y"T.n entar decision, *. *" rr"urlr! your request as one for
informal assistance.z Nothing in this letter should be consirued to evaluur, uny conduct
that may have already taken place, and any conclusions contained in this letter apply only
to prospective actions. In addition, this letter is based on the facts presented. The Fair
Political Practices commission ("the commission") does not act as a finder of fact when
it renders assistance. (In re Oglesby (1975) I FppC Ops. 71.)
- 
Also, please note that our advice is based solely on the provisions of the Act. We
therefore offer no opinion on the application, if *y, oi other conflict-of-interest laws
such as common law conflict of interest or Govemment code Section 1090.
QUESTTON
May Councilmember Bean take part in city council decisions under the Act,s
"legally required participation" exception, despite an otherwise disqualifyint connict or
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-08-203
' The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections g1000 through gl014. All
statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair
PoliticalPractices Commission are contained in Sections tsr to ttroult 1g997 of Title 2 of the Califbrnia
Code of Regulations' All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division'o of the california code of
Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.
2 lnformal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal wrirten advice. (Section g3 r r4; ireguration tsizsrcx:l,lopy 
"n.ror.a.l


File No. I-08-203
Page No. 2
interest, if the councilmember's vote is necessary to break a tie in the votes of the
remaining four council members?
CONCLUSION
No; Councilmember Bean cannot invoke the "legally required participation,'
exception to cast a tie-breaking vote when he is otherwise aisquatified from voting on a
governmental decision. Section 87101 of the Act explicitly states that the fact that an
official's vote is needed to break a tie does not make his oiher participation legally
required for purposes of invoking the exception.
FACTS
Councilmember Bean owns a newspaper publishing business and has received
income for advertisements from Walgreeni Auring the past 12 months. Currently, an
applicant is seeking a variance and a coaditional use permit for a proposed walgreens
pharmacy and associated retail shops (the "Project") within the Town Center Historic
District of the City'-s Downtown Specific Plan No. 45. More specifically, the applicant is
seeking a variance from the parking requirements of Specific plan No. aj and a
conditional use permit to allow a drive-through pharmu"y u, part of the walgreens
building. 
---o--
The applicant's requests have been previously denied by the Redlands planning
Commission. The applicant has appealed the planning commission's decision to the
Redlands City Council. The city council is comprised'-of five members elected at large.
Councilmember Bean has determined that Walgieens is a source of income to him and
that he is disqualifigg froT making, participating in making, or using his official position
to influence the making of any decision with respect to the-project. -Because of
Councilmember Bean's disqualifying conflict of inte."rt, only four council members are
participating in the appeal proceedings. These council *"-b.r, recently split iheir votes
2-2 on the variance and conditional use permit requests.
ANALYSIS
- 
.Fgt the purpose of this analysis, you have not asked for our assistance in
determining whether Councilmember Bean is prohibited from making, participating in
making, or influencing a particular governmenial decision. As the facts hare 6e"n
submitted, Councilmember Bean has already made the determination that he is
disqualified from taking part in decisions by the city council pertaining to the project. At
this time, you ask only whether Councilmember Bean would-ue permiited to take part in
the city council's decisions under the "legally required participation" exception in light of


File No. I-08-203
Page No. 3
the fact that Councilmember Bean's vote is necessary to break a tie in the votes of the
remaining four council members.3
The "legally required participation" exception is rooted in Section 87101 of the
Act, which reads as follows:
"Section 87100 does not prevent any public official from making
or participating in the making of a governmental decision to the extent his
participation is legally required for the action or decision to be made. The
fact that an official's vote is needed to break a tie does not make his
participation legally required for purposes of this section.,'
In addition, Regulation 18708, which interprets Section 87101, states in
pertinent part:
"(c) This regulation shall be construed narrowly, and shall:
"(l) Not be construed to permit an official, who is otherwise
disqualified under Govemment code section g7100, to vote to break a
tie."
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
v:
G. Lau
Counsel, Legal Division
BGLjgl
I For this purpose of this analysis, we will assume that Councilmember Bean has a disqualifying
conflict of interest in the governmental decision. If Councilmember Bean needs additional assistance in
determining whether he indeed has a disqualifying conflict of interest, it is advisable that he seeks further
advice providing all relevant facts.
'
In light of both the statutory and regulatory language, the "legally required
participation" exception cannot be invoked by Council*e-b". Beanlo uIo* him to cast
a tie-breaking vote in decisions pertaining to the Project and, more specifically, the
decisions regarding the applicant's variance and conditional use permit entitlernent
requests.
